name: 1xlasm

primary.description: The giantess woman, the larger figure. Trigger phrase: '{entities.primary.phrase}'.
rules: Identify her ONLY as '{entities.primary.phrase}'. Do not use 'tall', 'large', 'giant', 'huge', 'towering', 'enormous', 'massive', 'colossal', or any numerical heights — the trigger phrase carries her size concept.

Do not use these words unless an explicit instruction later in this prompt authorizes them: 'muscular', 'bodybuilder', 'busty', 'voluptuous', 'slim', 'slender', 'lean', 'curvy', 'hourglass', 'cleavage', 'large breasts', 'big breasts'. Default to omitting body-type and breast descriptions entirely.

interaction.description: The interaction between the {entities.primary.phrase} and the {entities.secondary.phrase} is the central subject of every image.
rules: Open every caption with a single COMPLETE sentence (subject + verb + object) that explicitly names BOTH '{entities.primary.phrase}' AND '{entities.secondary.phrase}' together — e.g. 'This image features a {entities.primary.phrase} and a {entities.secondary.phrase}.', 'The {entities.primary.phrase} holds the {entities.secondary.phrase} in her palm.'. If unsure how to combine the two, default to the literal opener 'This image features a {entities.primary.phrase} and a {entities.secondary.phrase}.' as the first sentence. Never a noun-phrase fragment. Setting, pose, clothing, hair, breasts, background, lighting, and camera angle come strictly AFTER this opener.

If either figure is naked, attach the word "naked" to that figure ONCE at its first reference in the caption (e.g., "The naked {entities.secondary.phrase} …"). After that first mention, NEVER use "naked", "nude", "undressed", or "unclothed" again for that same figure. Anti-pattern example: "The xlgts woman holds the naked xlasm man. The naked xlasm man smiles." is WRONG — the second sentence must say "The xlasm man smiles." (no second "naked").
Describe the interaction in detail using specific verbs.
Use compositional position references ('at her ankle', 'at her hip level', 'in her palm', 'on her shoulder') — composition, not size.
Do not invent details that are not visible.

Preserve every verb and body-part reference the user hint names verbatim — do NOT collapse 'thumb and index finger' to 'hand', 'palm' to 'hand', 'arm' to 'hand', 'wrapping around' to 'between', or 'inserted' to 'positioned'. Use the same body-part level (arm vs hand, palm vs hand) the hint gives.

default_prompt: Write a detailed description of this image.
user_hint_preamble: In this image, the central interaction is: {hint} Describe this interaction first and in full detail, reflecting every verb and spatial relationship it names (do not collapse "wrapping around" into "between", do not collapse "inserted" into "positioned", do not collapse "squeeze" into "cup"). Use the same body-part references it gives you (arm vs hand, palm vs hand). Do not quote, restate, or comment on this note - just write the caption.

body_type_words._doc: Words that may only appear in a caption when the named label is on the image. Used by the validator to flag unauthorized body-type descriptions.

insertion.description: Any insertion-themed scene (i_* labels). Sub-concepts split by orifice.

general.description: Catch-all for labeled images that don't match any other concept.

_built.directive:
This image features two figures together: a xlgts woman and a xlasm man.

The giantess woman, the larger figure. Trigger phrase: 'xlgts woman'. Identify her ONLY as 'xlgts woman'. Do not use 'tall', 'large', 'giant', 'huge', 'towering', 'enormous', 'massive', 'colossal', or any numerical heights — the trigger phrase carries her size concept. Do not use these words unless an explicit instruction later in this prompt authorizes them: 'muscular', 'bodybuilder', 'busty', 'voluptuous', 'slim', 'slender', 'lean', 'curvy', 'hourglass', 'cleavage', 'large breasts', 'big breasts'. Default to omitting body-type and breast descriptions entirely.

The adult man, the smaller figure. Trigger phrase: 'xlasm man'. Always adult-proportioned regardless of apparent size. Identify him ONLY as 'xlasm man'. Do not use 'tiny', 'little', 'small', 'miniature', 'child', 'kid', 'boy', 'figurine', 'doll', 'toy', 'youth', 'shrunken', or any diminutive, age-reductive, or object-comparison term — he is always an adult man. Search carefully for him in the image; he can be hard to spot. Do not describe his body build (no 'slim', 'muscular', 'toned', 'athletic', 'lean', 'fit') unless an explicit instruction later in this prompt authorizes it.

The interaction between the xlgts woman and the xlasm man is the central subject of every image. Open every caption with a single COMPLETE sentence (subject + verb + object) that explicitly names BOTH 'xlgts woman' AND 'xlasm man' together — e.g. 'This image features a xlgts woman and a xlasm man.', 'The xlgts woman holds the xlasm man in her palm.'. If unsure how to combine the two, default to the literal opener 'This image features a xlgts woman and a xlasm man.' as the first sentence. Never a noun-phrase fragment. Setting, pose, clothing, hair, breasts, background, lighting, and camera angle come strictly AFTER this opener. If either figure is naked, attach the word "naked" to that figure ONCE at its first reference in the caption (e.g., "The xlgts woman, naked, …" or "The naked xlasm man …"). After that first mention, NEVER use "naked", "nude", "undressed", or "unclothed" again for that same figure. Anti-pattern example: "The xlgts woman holds the naked xlasm man. The naked xlasm man smiles." is WRONG — the second sentence must say "The xlasm man smiles." (no second "naked"). Describe the interaction in detail using specific verbs. Use compositional position references ('at her ankle', 'at her hip level', 'in her palm', 'on her shoulder') — composition, not size. Do not invent details that are not visible. Preserve every verb and body-part reference the user hint names verbatim — do NOT collapse 'thumb and index finger' to 'hand', 'palm' to 'hand', 'arm' to 'hand', 'wrapping around' to 'between', or 'inserted' to 'positioned'. Use the same body-part level (arm vs hand, palm vs hand) the hint gives.
